structural_changes: Place timeline section before parties section
Remove header numbering and use ## formatting instead of # with numbered subsections
tone_changes: Use more concise, direct language without unnecessary descriptive phrases
Adopt formal legal document tone with precise terminology
content_additions: Include matter number and governing law in the matter overview
Add section references for contractual obligations
Include party designations as Respondent where applicable
content_removals: Remove witness information from parties table when not essential
Eliminate redundant clauses and obligations that don't add substantive value
Remove detailed execution information that doesn't impact core dispute
formatting_changes: Use warning symbols (⚠) to highlight unsupported claims
Consolidate related obligations into fewer, more focused items
Streamline parties table to essential roles only
learned_rules: Always include matter number and governing law in the opening overview
Prioritize chronological timeline over detailed party information in document structure
Focus obligations section on core contractual duties rather than comprehensive clause listing
Use section references when citing specific contractual provisions
Clearly mark any claims not supported by available documentation
Eliminate non-essential witness and execution details unless they relate to the dispute
